---

## Wave 3 TypeScript Review — Findings

### HIGH

**[HIGH] `src/pipeline/worker.ts:96` — `repo.fail()` called before `repo.pause()` on budget-pause path, incrementing `attempts`.**

`fail()` does `attempts = attempts + 1` unconditionally (job-repo.ts:70). The budget-pause branch at worker.ts:94-99 calls `repo.fail(job.id, errMsg)` then `repo.pause(job.id)`. This means every budget pause burns one attempt slot. After 3 daily-cap hits across retries the job transitions to poison and is permanently dead — the user never sees it re-queue when they `budget --reset`. Budget-pause is explicitly not a failure; `pause()` should be called directly without `fail()`. Fix: replace the two-call sequence with a single `repo.pause(job.id)` and a separate `repo.setLastError(job.id, errMsg)` (or add a `pauseWithNote()` that transitions state and records the message without touching `attempts`).

---

### MEDIUM

**[MEDIUM] `src/distill/stages/08-embed-index.ts:56` — non-1024 embedding silently dropped; loop continues with mismatched index.**

`if (!vec || vec.length !== 1024) { console.warn(...); continue; }` means the `results` array is shorter than `memories` — the caller receives fewer `IndexResult` entries than atoms passed in, with no indication which were dropped. The `PipelineResult.memoriesCreated + memoriesDeduped` count becomes wrong. Spec §14 says dim mismatch is a provider invariant violation; it should `throw new Error(...)` rather than silently skip, so the pipeline surfaces a misconfigured embed provider immediately.

**[MEDIUM] `src/distill/stages/06-reduce.ts:37` — 16-hex (64-bit) content hash truncation creates non-trivial collision risk at scale.**

`digest('hex').slice(0, 16)` gives 64 bits. With the birthday bound at ~2^32 (~4 billion) atom texts before a 50% collision probability, this is fine for a personal tool today but will silently merge unrelated memories as the corpus grows. The full 64-hex SHA-256 (256 bits) is free and the `Map` key cost is negligible. Drop the `.slice(0, 16)`.

---

### LOW

**[LOW] `src/budget/tracker.ts:98` — `Math.ceil(chars/4)` token approximation is undocumented at the call site.**

`estimateUsd` is used directly as a budget gate. The approximation is documented in the JSDoc on the method itself (good), but stage callers at compact.ts:63 and extract.ts:46 pass hardcoded `0.000002` per-token prices with no reference to where those come from or how stale they might become. A named constant (`AZURE_GPT4O_INPUT_COST_PER_TOKEN`) in a shared pricing file would make the approximation visible and auditable.

**[LOW] `src/pipeline/handlers/distill.ts:67` — dynamic `await import('node:crypto')` inside hot handler path.**

`createHash` is in Node.js core and never changes. The dynamic import is needless and adds a microtask per distill job. Use a top-level `import { createHash } from 'node:crypto'` instead.

---

### Items explicitly checked and cleared

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Budget checked BEFORE LLM call | Correct — `assertCanSpend` precedes every `provider.chat` call in both stages 4 and 5 |
| Concurrency race on budget | Not a risk — pipeline is strictly sequential (no `Promise.all`); single worker tick processes one job at a time |
| JSON-mode retry with stricter prompt | Correct — extract.ts retries exactly once with `EXTRACTION_STRICT_PROMPT`, then yields `[]` (bounded, no loop) |
| Zod on all LLM responses | Correct — `ExtractionSchema.safeParse` used; compact stage returns plain text (no schema needed) |
| Pipeline idempotency | Correct — stage 8 deduplicates by `finalHash` (SHA-256 of normalized text) before insert |
| Cleanup regex over-strips code blocks | No regex operates on code blocks; dedup only collapses N>=3 identical consecutive lines, harmless for code |
| Chunk turn-boundary respect | Correct — `TURN_BOUNDARY_RE` is applied only to start-of-line, oversized turns split by paragraph first |
| Memory-normalize entity dict | Well-bounded; applies only to known tech proper nouns with word-boundary anchors; not destructive to arbitrary content |
| `import type` discipline | Correct throughout; all type-only imports use `import type` |
| No `any`, no `as` casts, no `!` | Clean; DB results cast via typed `as X | undefined` after `.get()` (correct pattern for better-sqlite3) |
| `BudgetExceeded` re-export chain | Correct — pipeline.ts re-exports from tracker.ts; both stage aliases unify at the handler |

---

**Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-FIXES**

The HIGH item (budget-pause consuming attempt slots) will silently poison jobs after 3 daily budget hits and must be fixed before Wave 4. The two MEDIUMs are correctness risks worth addressing in the same pass. No CRITICAL type-unsafety or runtime boundary gaps found.
